political.s: Ford Motor Company (Lincoln's parent) reported left-leaning PAC donations with 0% going to Republican candidates out of 13,650 contributions according to FEC data. Ford resumed PAC donations in 2021 after a review and opted not to impose blanket bans on Republicans who voted against certifying the 2020 election.

charity.s: Ford Philanthropy (formerly Ford Fund) donated $73.7 million in 2023 and has contributed $2.3 billion cumulatively since 1949. The company supports disaster relief, food security, education, and entrepreneurship initiatives. During COVID-19, Ford donated over 120 million medical-grade masks to nonprofits and first responders.
charity.focus: Disaster relief, food security, education, entrepreneurship, community centers

environment.s: Ford achieved a 30% reduction in manufacturing CO2 emissions by 2018 (eight years ahead of 2025 target) through energy efficiency improvements and sustainable materials. The company committed to 100% renewable energy in manufacturing by 2035 and carbon neutrality by 2050, with aggressive electrification of vehicle lineup.

labor.s: Ford maintains union relationships with UAW and has zero-tolerance harassment policies. The company faces ongoing NLRB cases related to labor practices, though specific violations are not publicly disclosed. Executive compensation dramatically exceeds worker pay, with CEO-to-median employee ratio at 295:1 in 2025.

dei.s: Ford scaled back explicit DEI initiatives in 2024-2025, shifting language to 'inclusive culture' and withdrawing from HRC Corporate Equality Index. The company cited evolving legal environment as rationale. Previously maintained race-based ERGs and supplier diversity programs.

guns.s: Ford manufactures vehicles and does not directly sell firearms. However, Lincoln vehicles are purchased by government agencies and law enforcement who use them for armed services.

privacy.s: Ford/Lincoln collects extensive personal, vehicle, and location data through Connected Services. Mozilla Foundation rates privacy as poor due to extensive data collection, sharing with third parties including law enforcement without notice, and security vulnerabilities found in 2021 and 2023. Company declined to answer detailed privacy questions.

execPay.s: CEO Jim Farley earned $27.5 million in 2025 (295:1 ratio to median employee pay of $93,397), up 11% from 2024. Compensation heavily weighted toward stock awards (76%) and performance bonuses tied to quality and software metrics, despite company leading industry in recall volume.

fec.committeeName: FORD LINCOLN-MERCURY MINORITY DEALERS ASSOCIATION POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE (FLMMDA PAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
